JUb19 versus CEN2ent

Based on May 7th Discussion, JUb19 and CEN2ent became a main interest, and we now conducted the competition experiment under different initial frequencies. This allowed us to estimate competition coefficients via a different approach; thus, we give it a separate treatment here.

Data formating from pairwise competition

Compile data from different competition assays (JUb19 as donor and CEN2ent species as recipient) at start (0 hr) and end (24 hr) points (under OD = 1).

Frequency lineplot (JUb19 vs CEN2ent)

Here, as a first attempt to visualize the competitive outcome, we plot the frequency change across 0 and 24 hrs. The first figure shows the frequency change for JUb19 (donor) and the second mirrored figure shows that for CEN2ent (recipient). Overall, there’s a signature of species coexistence: the rare species increase in frequency whereas the abundant species decreases in frequency.

Interspecies coefficient alpha (JUb19 vs CEN2ent)

Again, consider a Lotkta–Volterra competition model in the form of \(\frac{dN_i}{dt} = N_i(r_i-\alpha_{ii}N_i-\alpha_{ij}N_j)\). Previously, we estimated the interspecific competition coefficients (\(\alpha_{ij}\)) by rearranging the growth expression. That is: \[\alpha_{ij} = \left[\hat{r_i}-\hat{\alpha_{ii}}N_{i0}^c-\left(\frac{lnN_{i24}^c - lnN_{i0}^c}{24})\right)\right].\left(\frac{1}{N_{j0}^c}\right)\] Here, however, since we have a large initial density gradient for the competition between JUb19 and CEN2ent, we can apply the same statistical fitting approach used to obtained the intraspecific competition coefficients. We did this is a “sequential” approach: the response variable is the difference between the observed per capita growth rate (\(\frac{\Delta lnN_i}{\Delta t}\)) and the intraspecific competitive impact (i.e., \(\hat{r_i}-\hat{\alpha_{ii}}N_{i0}^c\)), termed the “residual” in the following figures. This residual term is modeled as a function of the initial heterospecific competitor density (\(N_{j0}^c\)), and the resulting slope represents the interspecific competition coefficient. Note again all density data were scaled by \(10^9\) prior to calculation. Below, we show (left) the estimated effect of CEN2ent on JUb19 (\(\alpha_{\textrm{JUb19, CEN2ent}}\)), and (right) the estimated effect of JUb19 on CEN2ent (\(\alpha_{\textrm{CEN2ent, JUb19}}\)). A more detailed comparison of this approach and the previous one, as well as the simulated over competitive outcome, will follow soon.
